prior_session_note: S108 PLAN-ONLY session (team seat hit weekly limit ~1% remaining — owner: 'we are not going to work anything this batch ... update all backlog ... save the open plans and pdr so we can continue working next session in another claude account'). Owner playtested live S107 and reported 6 points. We: (1) ran a clean pre-flight + dispositioned all 5 WARNs; (2) scoped all 6 points against the code via a 6-investigator Opus workflow (wf_6c9fd480-8a2); (3) deliberated 2 Council rounds (Grok-4.20 + Gemini-2.5-pro) + PRIME-AUDIT; (4) split into 4 risk-tiered batches and persisted READY plans. NO code shipped. Next session (possibly a different seat) starts by executing Batch A.
council_result: S108 planning. 6-investigator Opus workflow mapped all 6 owner points to exact files/symbols + wire/determinism. Council R1 (Grok REJECT / Gemini ADOPT-WITH-CHANGES) headlined a MANDATORY PROTOCOL_VERSION 12->13 bump claiming the client predicts creature/spark physics. PRIME-AUDIT REFUTED this against the code: main.ts:1055-1075 ('the client runs no authoritative physics'); save.ts:460 trimMirrorCreature strips AI, client rehydrates neutral; creatureRenderer.ts:177-183 derives a render-only velocity estimate from synced positions; lerp.ts is position-clamp not velocity-extrapolation; PROTOCOL_VERSION has been 12 since S103 (a WIRE change) while S107 sim changes (leader-decay) shipped without a bump. => host-only sim changes with unchanged wire FORMAT need NO bump. Adopted all real Council findings (TTL-drop reset, poop-always-terminates, test/lock updates, spawner burst-avoidance). Council R2 (deltas) both ADOPT-WITH-CHANGES; rejected Grok's poop max-lifetime net (redundant - floor is an unconditional y-threshold) and Grok's 'keep Helga range, hide beam' (masks the visual but keeps the cross-map hit the owner is complaining about).

priorities.label: S108 Batch A PDR (READY, deliberated, owner go received) — #5 codex Esc/toggle + #6 shape 10s-TTL-despawn (NO clamp; fling is an owner tactic) + #1 poop model (disable fouled turret/spawner; slow chewer/Voltkin on hit + Helga slap-cadence when fouled; carried-spark 50% slow; idle pool immune; foul placed prims = no points) + #3 Helga anti-cross-map-laser INTERIM (range 2203->~380 + remove beam streak). PROTOCOL_VERSION 12 unchanged.
priorities.check_method: RALPH:PATROL per-priority on each diff + full backpressure sweep. P0 (codex Esc/G+C toggle): Escape handler guarded by codexOverlay.isVisible(), standalone listener returns so no double-handle with settingsOverlay's DOM-root Escape; G+C close bypasses chordBlocked() (close always safe); codexChordFired latch prevents key-repeat re-toggle; UI-only, tsc 0. P1 (10s TTL reap): reap collects expired[] before dispatching (no mid-iteration mutation), Free-only guard + applyDespawnSpark non-Free no-op = carry/bond never yanked, createdTick reset on drop (fresh window), pure tick math = replay-safe; +8 tests (7 reap + 1 drop-reset), full save.replay/stateHash suite green. P2 (poop rework): new precedence avatar->creature->structure->carried-spark->floor; idle Free immune (pass-through+terminate-at-floor tests), only Carried slowed; creature poopyUntilTick host-only (serializeCreature whitelist never emits it) -> NO-OP byte-identical when un-pooped (Voltkin/chewer replay guard); turret held IDLE while fouled + resumes no-burst; +9 tests; spawner-emit fouled-skip in main.ts has no unit harness (mirrors tested turret path) - flagged. P3 (Helga interim): range 2203->380 (no cross-map hit - test enemy@3252px unreached), drawSlapReach beam removed (drawHelga->void), princess slap-cadence 2x stretch when fouled byte-identical unfouled (Math.round(n*1)===n); +1 test. CHECK gates: tsc 0, vitest 1702/1702 (101->102 files, +18 net new tests), npm run build entry 597.5/750 KiB (152.5 headroom), PROTOCOL_VERSION 12 unchanged (no wire-format change - host-sim/UI/render only), 4 independent commits e2c8500/4ef506a/08bdfbd/9259cd1 each deploy SUCCESS (P3 deploy in-flight at write).

reflexion_entries_to_archive: S108-PLAN #verify-the-councils-confident-consensus-against-the-actual-architecture: a 3-way Council (Grok+Gemini, both premium-reasoning models) UNANIMOUSLY headlined a MANDATORY PROTOCOL_VERSION bump, asserting the client predicts creature/spark physics so a host-only slow/clamp would desync. PRIME-AUDIT refuted it against the code: the SPARK client runs ZERO authoritative physics (main.ts:1055 'the client runs no authoritative physics'; creatures stripped of AI + rendered from synced positions; lerp is position-clamp not velocity-extrapolation) and PROTOCOL_VERSION bumps only on WIRE-FORMAT change (12 since S103; S107 sim changes shipped without a bump). LESSON: two models converging is NOT independent confirmation when they share a generic prior (here: 'games use predict-and-reconcile netcode'); the convergence can be a shared wrong assumption. Always verify a confident consensus claim against the actual codebase before adopting it - but STILL harvest the peripheral findings (the Council's TTL-drop edge case, poop-termination, and test/lock updates were all real and adopted).

S108-PLAN #owner-corrections-reshape-scope-mid-plan-keep-the-PDR-living: the owner refined the spec three times across the session (no velocity clamp - the fast-fling is an intended tactic; Helga reads as a cross-map laser; poop must not touch idle spawn-zone shapes but the carried-spark 50% slow is intended). Each correction was folded into a living PDR (REV1->REV3) with a focused delta re-deliberation rather than a from-scratch redo. Two of the three corrections REDUCED risk (dropped the contested clamp; simplified poop precedence). LESSON: when the owner is actively spec-ing, treat the PDR as a living doc, re-deliberate only the DELTA, and confirm the genuinely-ambiguous fork (carried-vs-free spark) explicitly rather than guessing - but when the owner declines to pick, commit to the reading their own words point at and make it easy to redirect at the go-gate.

S109-PLAN-A #host-only-field-needs-no-wire-bump-when-the-serializer-is-a-whitelist: Batch A shipped 4 host-sim/UI priorities with PROTOCOL_VERSION held at 12 (the S108 deliberation's load-bearing claim, refuting the Council's 'mandatory bump'). The cleanest empirical confirmation came at implementation: adding Creature.poopyUntilTick needed ZERO save.ts changes because serializeCreature is an explicit field WHITELIST (no object spread) - an unknown field simply never reaches the wire. LESSON: before assuming a new entity field forces a protocol bump, READ the serializer - a whitelist serializer makes host-only runtime fields free (no wire surface, rehydrate-neutral on load like targetCreatureId). The byte-equivalence guard is then a unit test that the un-pooped path returns identical output (Math.round(n*1)===n for the cadence stretch; NO-OP scaling for the steering accel), backed by the full save.replay/stateHash suite. 1702/1702 green, entry 597.5/750 KiB, 4 independent revertible commits.

S109-PLAN-A #the-unlock-hook-vocabulary-vs-the-final-gate-vocabulary: setting the PDR gate, the UserPromptSubmit unlock hook (glue_pdr_unlock) wrote priority_state:'unlocked' + unlock_source:'user', but pdca-final-gate.sh only ACCEPTS priority_state in {approved,in_progress,completed} - so the first Edit was blocked with 'priority_state=unlocked. Approve PDR before editing' despite a clean user 'go'. FIX: align priority_state to 'in_progress' (the priority genuinely IS in progress; unlock_source:user is the load-bearing attribution field, already minted by the hook). LESSON: the unlock-writer and the write-gate use different state vocabularies (a known WS1 debt - the v3 priority_state path); when a legit user-unlocked edit is blocked on priority_state, reconcile it to the gate's accepted set in session-state (allowlisted file) rather than re-prompting the user.
